attack_vector: The definitions and theorems provided strictly formalize the cardinalities of finite function spaces (TMTapeConfig and FRSB_Basin). No computational complexity bounds or algorithmic impossibility claims are made yet, so there are no algorithmic bypasses or Graveyard violations present in this purely foundational step. The mathematical logic is sound and merely sets up finite combinatorial bounds.
director_pivot_advice: Proceed with the proof construction, but be extremely cautious in the next steps. You have established that the total number of basins is 2^n and the tape configurations scale as |Γ|^S. You cannot use this to argue that a TM requires 2^n tape size to explore the basins; a TM can iteratively search 2^n elements using only O(n) space. Any attempt to directly bind the TM's tape entropy to the total cardinality of the search space to prove a lower bound will violate the Graveyard rules (specifically Global Topology/Volume and Dimensionality Constraints). Focus on querying/path limits rather than total memory.
